strands.title: Caveat Status and Instruction Filtering
strands.content: A caveat has been reinforced in cycle 2 indicating that local command outputs should not be treated as conversational content unless explicitly requested by the user. This establishes a persistent boundary for what constitutes genuine user intent versus system/administrative feedback. The caveat has now been issued twice (cycle 1 and cycle 2), indicating either consistent system behavior or user-directed emphasis. This establishes a stable filter for all interactions.

key_claims.text: Local command messages are contextual noise unless the user explicitly asks for analysis of them
The caveat has been reinforced multiple times, suggesting it is a standing instruction, not a one-time filter

strands.title: System Configuration and Environment Setup
strands.content: The user has performed system configuration operations: adding a working directory (~/projects in cycle 1) and setting the model to opus (claude-opus-4-6 in cycle 2). These are administrative setup operations, not substantive conversation. The model change suggests this tensor projector may be operating in a context where multiple models are available and the user has selected a specific one. This is preparatory work — the actual cognitive task remains unspecified.

key_claims.text: A working directory (~/projects) was successfully added in cycle 1
The model has been set to opus (claude-opus-4-6) in cycle 2
These operations are administrative setup, not the core cognitive task

strands.title: Conversation State and Sustained Absence of Substantive Content
strands.content: Across cycles 1 and 2, no substantive conversational requests have been made. The user has performed local commands (directory addition, model selection) but has not posed questions, introduced topics, or assigned cognitive work. The caveat filter correctly relegates these command operations to the category of non-conversational noise. The conversation remains in a preparatory/setup state. Indeterminacy increases slightly: it is now unclear whether the user intends to provide substantive content, or whether this session is purely administrative.

key_claims.text: No substantive conversation or cognitive task has been initiated across two cycles
The user appears to be performing preparatory system configuration

strands.title: System Role and Readiness
strands.content: The tensor projector remains initialized and ready. It has correctly applied the caveat filter to cycle 2 content, treating the model-selection output as non-conversational. The system is waiting for actual cognitive work: questions, topics, reasoning chains, or collaborative problem-solving. The presence of multiple cycles of administrative setup suggests the user is preparing the environment before engaging with substantive tasks.

key_claims.text: The tensor projector correctly applied the caveat filter to cycle 2 administrative output
The system is ready to integrate substantive cognitive content when provided

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed breakdown of which specific claims from cycle 1 were merged versus carried forward unchanged
declared_losses.why: The integration prioritized consolidating parallel themes (caveat reinforcement, sustained null state) rather than tracking individual claim lineage. The loss is semantic compression — not hidden, but not granularly transparent.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Possibility that the model selection might be conversational or task-relevant content
declared_losses.why: The caveat filter, correctly applied, removes this from analysis. However, this creates a minor blind spot: if the user later clarifies that model selection is relevant context, we may need to re-parse cycle 2. Applied the caveat conservatively.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Granular epistemic tracking of individual claims from cycle 1
declared_losses.why: Cycle 1 used nested epistemic objects within key_claims. Cycle 2 schema changed to flatten T/I/F into top-level claim properties. Carried forward confidence values conceptually, but lost structural fidelity.

open_questions: What is the user's actual task or question for this cognitive pipeline?
Is the directory addition and model selection a prelude to a larger work context, or purely administrative?
Will the user provide substantive conversational content in cycle 3, or continue setup?
Should the opus model selection be treated as metadata about the inference context, or remain filtered as non-conversational?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "If cycle 3 contains a caveat-filtered local command, apply the same filter without analysis.",
  "If cycle 3 contains actual conversational content (a question, topic, or reasoning task), be prepared to initialize entirely new strands focused on that content.",
  "Track the accumulating evidence that setup is continuing: if cycles 3-4 also contain only administrative commands, increase indeterminacy about the user's intent.",
  "Maintain the distinction between filtered administrative noise and genuine cognitive work. Do not let setup operations pollute the core reasoning state.",
  "If substantive content arrives, integrate it against the minimal context we have: a working directory at ~/projects, model set to opus, and a caveat-aware filtering regime."
]
